highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.101 Released
highlights.summary: The latest release adds a `/team-onboarding` command that automatically generates a ramp-up guide for new teammates based on your local Claude Code usage — great for teams onboarding developers quickly. It also adds support for OS CA certificate stores by default, meaning enterprise users behind TLS proxies no longer need extra configuration to get Claude Code working.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.98: Interactive Google Vertex AI Setup Wizard
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.98 introduces a step-by-step interactive wizard for connecting Claude Code to Google Vertex AI, accessible directly from the login screen under '3rd-party platform'. The wizard walks you through GCP authentication, project and region selection, credential verification, and model pinning — making it much easier for teams using Google Cloud infrastructure.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.97: Focus View Toggle and Status Line Improvements
highlights.summary: This release adds a focus view toggle (Ctrl+O) in NO_FLICKER mode that shows a clean view of your prompt, a one-line tool summary with edit diff stats, and the final response — helpful for reducing visual noise during long tasks. A new `refreshInterval` setting for the status line lets you automatically re-run status commands on a schedule, and git worktree info is now available in the status line.

highlights.title: Claude Code Found a Linux Vulnerability Hidden for 23 Years
highlights.summary: A developer used Claude Code to discover a Linux vulnerability that had gone undetected for 23 years, showcasing the tool's potential for deep codebase analysis beyond everyday coding tasks. This is a compelling real-world example of how AI-assisted code review can surface subtle, long-standing security issues that human reviewers might miss.

highlights.title: Anthropic Ending Claude Subscription Support for Third-Party Harnesses Like OpenClaw
highlights.summary: Starting April 4, Anthropic stopped allowing Claude subscription usage limits to be applied to third-party harnesses such as OpenClaw — these tools now require separate, additional usage beyond your subscription. If you've been using OpenClaw or similar tools with your Claude account, you'll need to review your billing setup and may face additional costs.

highlights.title: Depwire – Codebase Dependency Graph and MCP Server for AI Coding Assistants
highlights.summary: Depwire is an open-source tool that generates a visual dependency graph of your codebase and exposes it as an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, giving AI coding assistants like Claude Code richer context about how your code is structured. This can help Claude Code make more accurate suggestions when working across large or complex codebases with many interconnected modules.

highlights.title: Show HN: Give Your AI Coding Assistant Persistent, Semantic Memory
highlights.summary: Total Recall is an open-source project that adds persistent, semantic memory to AI coding assistants, allowing Claude Code and similar tools to remember context and decisions across sessions. For developers working on long-running projects, this addresses one of the biggest pain points — having to re-explain your codebase or conventions every time you start a new conversation.

tips_of_the_day: If your team is onboarding a new developer, run `/team-onboarding` in Claude Code v2.1.101+ to auto-generate a personalized ramp-up guide based on your actual project usage — it saves hours of documentation work.

Before starting a long multi-file refactoring session in Claude Code, briefly describe your coding conventions and architecture at the start of the conversation (or use a persistent memory tool) so Claude doesn't make inconsistent decisions across files.

If you're behind a corporate firewall or VPN and Claude Code keeps failing with TLS errors, upgrade to v2.1.101 — it now trusts your OS certificate store by default, which resolves most enterprise proxy issues without any manual configuration.
